meta.cta: Get longer-lasting sets with Rubber Base Gel from Crystal Clawz
meta.painPoint: Your gel keeps popping off after three days.

workflowSummary: Create Jenn avatar, product, and scene images first, upload them into one Google Flow project, tag assets in Gemini Agent Mode, generate one clip at a time, then edit clips conversationally.

referenceAssetPrompts.title: Jenn avatar image
referenceAssetPrompts.useIn: Create this in Gemini image, then upload it to Flow as Jenn's avatar reference.
referenceAssetPrompts.prompt: Create a realistic reference image that can be reused later in Flow / Gemini Agent Mode. Vertical 9:16 iPhone-style image of a South African woman in her 30s, professional but approachable, seated at a nail station, natural makeup, wearing a black apron. She looks like a real South African nail educator, clear, practical, and teaching-focused, natural expression, candid but neat, no celebrity likeness, no brand logos on clothing.

referenceAssetPrompts.title: Product image
referenceAssetPrompts.useIn: Create this in Gemini image, then upload it to Flow as the product reference.
referenceAssetPrompts.prompt: Create a realistic reference image that can be reused later in Flow / Gemini Agent Mode. Vertical 9:16 product reference image for Rubber Base Gel on a salon desk setup. The product is clear and central, salon lighting, practical UGC style, no competitor branding, no copied packaging style, no fake official label.

referenceAssetPrompts.title: Scene image
referenceAssetPrompts.useIn: Create this in Gemini image, then upload it to Flow as the scene reference.
referenceAssetPrompts.prompt: Create a realistic reference image that can be reused later in Flow / Gemini Agent Mode. Vertical 9:16 scene reference image of Neat nail station with tools, lamp, and products visible. Warm ring light or natural window light from the left. Natural South African salon content feel, tidy but real, no people in frame, leave space for Rubber Base Gel.

flowSetupChecklist: Create the Jenn avatar, product, and scene images in Gemini image
Open Google Flow and create a new project for this campaign
Upload the generated Jenn avatar image as @jenn_avatar
Upload the generated product image as @rubber-base-gel
Upload the generated scene image as @scene_reference
Switch to Gemini / Agent Mode before generating clips
Turn on confirm before generating so credits are not spent by accident
In each clip prompt, tag @jenn_avatar and @rubber-base-gel
Generate one clip at a time and keep the same conversation open
hooks: If your gel keeps popping off after three days, watch this before your next set.
Nail techs struggling with lifting, this Rubber Base Gel tip will save you a lot of frustration.
Still battling with your gel keeps popping off after three days? Start with your foundation.
Rubber Base Gel is not just another product - it fixes a real salon problem.
Problem/Solution idea: show the problem, show the fix, then show the result.

clipScript.action: Open with the pain point while the product is visible nearby.
clipScript.spokenLine: If your gel keeps popping off after three days, check the foundation before you blame the colour.
clipScript.visualNote: Neat nail station with tools, lamp, and products visible. Keep Rubber Base Gel visible without making the shot feel like a hard sell.
clipScript.referenceAssetNote: Use a clean product image on a salon desk or in a hand close-up

clipScript.action: Show the frustrating moment or common mistake in a simple way.
clipScript.spokenLine: Most lifting issues come from prep, product control, or the layer being too thick.
clipScript.visualNote: Show the problem clearly: your gel keeps popping off after three days. Keep the shot simple and realistic.
clipScript.referenceAssetNote: Photograph your station before filming - use it as a reference image in Flow

clipScript.action: Show the product as the practical fix and explain the key step.
clipScript.spokenLine: With Rubber Base Gel, the key is thin layers, clean prep, and proper control.
clipScript.visualNote: Show Rubber Base Gel being used neatly with a clear view of the amount and texture.
clipScript.referenceAssetNote: Show the product clearly in at least 2 clips

clipScript.action: Show the result and end with a clear next step.
clipScript.spokenLine: Get longer-lasting sets with Rubber Base Gel from Crystal Clawz. Save this for your next set.
clipScript.visualNote: Show a clean finished result with the product still visible.
clipScript.referenceAssetNote: Use the same product, person, and setup so the clip feels continuous.

agentModePrompts.title: Initial Flow / Gemini prompt
agentModePrompts.prompt: Create a vertical UGC video, 8 seconds. Use @jenn_avatar as Jenn and @rubber-base-gel as the product reference. She is in a salon desk setup: Neat nail station with tools, lamp, and products visible. Open with the pain point while the product is visible nearby. She speaks directly to the lens with a handheld iPhone feel, soft natural light, slightly candid movement. Spoken line: "If your gel keeps popping off after three days, check the foundation before you blame the colour." Keep her style: Confident South African nail educator. Warm, direct, knowledgeable. Tone: clear, practical, and teaching-focused. Show the product clearly in at least 2 clips.
agentModePrompts.editingPrompt: Keep everything the same, including @jenn_avatar, @rubber-base-gel, the voice, framing, and timing, but make the opening line more direct.

agentModePrompts.title: Agent Mode follow-up clip 2
agentModePrompts.prompt: Jenn continues in the same Flow conversation. Use @jenn_avatar and @rubber-base-gel. Same woman, same salon desk, same handheld UGC style. Show the frustrating moment or common mistake in a simple way. Spoken line: "Most lifting issues come from prep, product control, or the layer being too thick." Keep continuity from the previous clip and keep Rubber Base Gel recognisable.
agentModePrompts.editingPrompt: Keep everything the same, including @jenn_avatar, @rubber-base-gel, the voice, framing, and timing, but show more of the product.

agentModePrompts.title: Agent Mode follow-up clip 3
agentModePrompts.prompt: Jenn continues in the same Flow conversation. Use @jenn_avatar and @rubber-base-gel. Same woman, same salon desk, same handheld UGC style. Show the product as the practical fix and explain the key step. Spoken line: "With Rubber Base Gel, the key is thin layers, clean prep, and proper control." Keep continuity from the previous clip and keep Rubber Base Gel recognisable.
agentModePrompts.editingPrompt: Keep everything the same, including @jenn_avatar, @rubber-base-gel, the voice, framing, and timing, but make the application step clearer.

agentModePrompts.title: Agent Mode follow-up clip 4
agentModePrompts.prompt: Jenn continues in the same Flow conversation. Use @jenn_avatar and @rubber-base-gel. Same woman, same salon desk, same handheld UGC style. Show the result and end with a clear next step. Spoken line: "Get longer-lasting sets with Rubber Base Gel from Crystal Clawz. Save this for your next set." Keep continuity from the previous clip and keep Rubber Base Gel recognisable.
agentModePrompts.editingPrompt: Keep everything the same, including @jenn_avatar, @rubber-base-gel, the voice, framing, and timing, but make the final result brighter and neater.

editingPrompts: Keep everything the same, including @jenn_avatar and @rubber-base-gel, but make Rubber Base Gel more visible in the first two clips.
Keep everything exactly the same, but make Jenn's spoken line simpler and more natural for South African nail techs.
Keep everything exactly the same, but change only the background detail or clothing colour requested. Do not change Jenn, the product, voice, or product reference.
caption: Rubber Base Gel for nail techs who want cleaner, longer-lasting sets
cta: Get longer-lasting sets with Rubber Base Gel from Crystal Clawz
assetChecklist: Gemini image Jenn avatar image created and uploaded as @jenn_avatar
Gemini image product image created and uploaded as @rubber-base-gel
Gemini image scene image created and uploaded as @scene_reference
Confirm before generating is enabled in Flow
All clips are generated inside the same Flow project conversation
safetyChecklist: Do not use celebrity likenesses, actors, or influencers in prompts
Do not reference competitor brands or their visual styles
Do not copy recognisable branded aesthetics, colours, fonts, or packaging
Use only original Crystal Clawz assets, generic AI avatars, or consent-based real people
Commercial content must not imply endorsements from public figures
Review all AI-generated output before publishing
If using Jenn's real likeness: ensure consent is documented before publishing commercially
